title: set_actor_on_path adds non-retail patrol-init writes (lastvisible60=timer, idle-anim call)

history.note: Retail set_actor_on_path at 0x7F0290F8 initializes the patrol union with actiontype=ACT_PATROL, forward=1, nextstep, path, waydata.age=rand%100, waydata.unk03=0, then writes -1 to self offset 0x78 at 0x7F029228 and speed=0.0 at self offset 0x7C, and never loads g_GlobalTimer nor calls an idle-animation helper. The NATIVE_PORT body reproduces the patrol-step search and those struct writes faithfully, but adds two non-retail statements: an unconditional lastvisible60=g_GlobalTimer init and a chrlvIdleAnimationRelated call. Both are documented in-code as compensations for port-side fog culling being too aggressive and for a camera-anim state mismatch. They are always-on under NATIVE_PORT with no negative-control flag. The removed non-native padding index 0x13 write is a justified 64-bit adaptation (on N64 it aliases a field that is immediately overwritten; on 64-bit it would corrupt waydata.age).
